title: Scalar Lax-Friedrichs weights sum to one

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Volume Methods for Hyperbolic Problems, Cambridge University Press, 2002, Sec. 12.5, p. 232.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For any real CFL number cfl, the scalar Lax-Friedrichs weights (1 + cfl) / 2 and (1 - cfl) / 2 sum to 1.
informal.latex: For any \(\lambda\in\mathbb{R}\), the Lax-Friedrichs weights satisfy \((1+\lambda)/2+(1-\lambda)/2=1\).
informal.expected_conclusion: The two scalar Lax-Friedrichs weights sum to one.

declarations.name: laxFriedrichsWeights_sum
declarations.statement: theorem laxFriedrichsWeights_sum (cfl : ℝ) : laxFriedrichsLeftWeight cfl + laxFriedrichsRightWeight cfl = 1

tasks.input: Translate the scalar Lax-Friedrichs weight-sum identity into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem laxFriedrichsWeights_sum (cfl : ℝ) : laxFriedrichsLeftWeight cfl + laxFriedrichsRightWeight cfl = 1

tasks.input: Given the weight definitions and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof using unfold and ring.
